results.title: Nicholas and Alexandra: prelude to revolution, 1904-1905
results.creator_credit: Franklin J. Schaffner, Sam Spiegel and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description:
Episode number 3 of Series “Western Civilization”.
Focuses on the years 1904-1905. Shows some of the causes of the Russian revolution that were visible more than a decade before Lenin arrived at the Finland station. Reveals the extreme contrasts of Russia, the role of the Czar, the workers, the revolutionaries and the peasants. Uses excerpts from the film “Nicholas and Alexandra (1971) starring Michael Jayston, Janet Suzman, Laurence Olivier.

results.title: Bismarck: Germany from blood and iron
results.creator_credit: John irvin, Malcolm B. Heyworth, Chatsworth Film Productions and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Western Civilization”.
Nationalism is a major force in modern history, and national unity has often been forged in war. This film traces the step-by-step creation of modern Germany and suggests its implications for the rest of the world. In 1815 there were thirty-nine separate German-speaking states in a confederation under Austrian leadership. By 1871 a united German nation was born, led by the northern state of Prussia and the man who was to be its first Chancellor - Otto Von Bismarck. Relaxing with two American friends, the architect of the new Germany recollects the steps which led up to unification, which is told in a series of flashbacks. Suitable for middle and upper secondary levels.

results.title: A Matter of conscience: Henry VIII and Sir Thomas More
results.creator_credit: Fred Zimmerman and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Western Civilization”.
An edited version of ‘A Man for All Seasons’ concentrating on the historic clash between Henry VIII and Sir Thomas More as a prototypical confrontation between the power of the state and the individual who chooses to oppose it.

results.title: Jumping molecules!
results.creator_credit: Christopher Haws, Michael Latham and Landmark Films
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Start Here: Adventures into Science”.
Konrad, the kitchen robot, shows how the energy in heat may be used to demonstrate the fundamentals of physics. Focuses on temperature and presents experiments using common household materials involving the kitchen refrigerator, cold water and hot water.

results.title: Make a noise
results.creator_credit: Christopher Haws, Michael Latham and Landmark Films
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Start Here: Adventures into Science”.
Explains what sound is and why some sounds are musical. Shows how to experiment with vibrations and to create music. This programme is suitable for upper primary science.

results.title: The Invisible force
results.creator_credit: Christopher Haws, Michael Latham and Landmark Films
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Start Here: Adventures into Science”.
Konrad, the kitchen robot, shows students that gravity and friction determine how everything on earth behaves during movement. Using simple experiments that can be reproduced in class or at home, he reveals the nature of the forces acting upon us.

results.title: Mix a material
results.creator_credit: Christopher Haws, Michael Latham, Landmark Films and Video Arts
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Start Here: Adventures into Science”.
Looks at why materials bend, stretch and break. Demonstrates experiments to show the behaviour of molecular lattices in everyday substances from chocolate to rubber ballons.

results.title: The Amazing magnet
results.creator_credit: Christopher Haws, Michael Latham and Video Arts
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Start Here: Adventures into Science”.
This program presents a range of experiments that demonstrate the forces of magnets. Illustrates the nature and properties of magnetic fields, briefly traces the history of the compass, shows how the earth behaves like a magnet and presents some modern applications of electro- magnets.

Episode of Series “Start Here: Adventures into Science”.
This program presents experiments that demonstrate the varying forces that are created when air moves. Demonstrates the theory of flight and the creation of weather patterns.
